qwq-32b.text: Okay, let's tackle this case.
The patient is a 54-year-old man with facial flushing and diarrhea that started 7 weeks ago.
The symptoms began intermittently but are now constant.
The key lab finding here is elevated 5-HIAA in the urine.
I remember that 5-HIAA is a breakdown product of serotonin.
So, high levels of this usually point to a condition where too much serotonin is being produced.
Serotonin is typically made by enterochromaffin cells in the gut, but also by neuroendocrine cells.
When there's an excess, it's often due to a neuroendocrine tumor, like a carcinoid tumor.
Carcinoid syndrome is the name for the symptoms caused by these tumors, right?
The classic symptoms include flushing (which he has), diarrhea, maybe wheezing, and right heart valve lesions in some cases.

The elevated 5-HIAA is a big clue.
